preview_text:
The model favors Mexico heavily at 82%, leaving South Africa with only a 1% chance of victory. This asymmetry reflects a strong expected performance gap between the two sides. By contrast, the sportsbook consensus gives South Africa substantially more room—nearly 14% to win—a meaningful difference in how the underdog is valued.

The model's forecast is dominated by a Mexican win; the draw at 17% is its second-most-likely outcome. This distribution suggests a competitive match where a stalemate remains plausible, but Mexico is expected to exert sufficient control or clinical finishing to secure three points most often. The South African path to victory, in the model's view, is narrow.

Model and market diverge noticeably. The gap on a Mexico win is about 18 percentage points—the model is substantially more confident in Mexico. The sportsbook assigns South Africa more than nine times the model's win probability, and prices the draw 6 percentage points higher. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects the true match odds. Polymarket data is not yet available.
